source_jobs.title: Vendor & Partnership Manager
source_jobs.description:
ABOUT FORMA

The employee benefits landscape needs a reset. Companies invest millions annually in benefits that employees neither value nor use regularly. Forma, founded in 2017, set out to challenge this one-size-fits-all approach.

Forma's global lifestyle benefits platform enables companies to offer competitive packages while reducing costs and inefficiencies. While giving employees more choice and flexibility in spending their benefit allowances, the Forma platform saves HR, total rewards, and benefits professionals countless hours they would have spent managing and supporting multiple point solutions.

With Forma, companies can choose from a suite of products, including Lifestyle Spending Accounts, Health Savings Accounts, Health Reimbursement Arrangements, Flexible Spending Accounts, and more. These products allow companies to design and deliver customized benefits programs through a single platform. Employees can then spend account funds in three ways: The Forma Store, The Forma Visa Card, or claim reimbursement.

Forma has helped hundreds of renowned companies, including Stripe, UKG, lululemon, and Tripadvisor, to design equitable, scalable, and flexible benefits programs for nearly 1 million employees worldwide.

Forma is backed by Emergence Capital and Ribbit Capital. We have received numerous awards for our exponential growth, innovation, and as a "Great Place to Work."




ABOUT THE ROLE

Forma is redefining what employee benefits look like. We believe benefits shouldn't live in a PDF — they should feel like a curated experience. As our Vendor & Partnership Manager, you'll be the architect of that experience, building and managing a best-in-class catalog of vendors and partners that bring our members' benefits to life.

This is a rare blend of merchandising instinct, relationship-building skill, and operational rigor.. You'll own the full vendor lifecycle — from sourcing and negotiating to onboarding and ongoing performance — ensuring every brand in the Forma store earns its place and delivers real value to our members.




YOU WILL

Partnership & Vendor Management

 - Source, evaluate, and onboard new vendor and brand partners aligned with Forma's member demographics and benefit categories

 - Negotiate commercial terms including pricing, exclusives, revenue share, and co-marketing opportunities

 - Build and maintain strong long-term relationships with vendor partners, serving as their primary point of contact

 - Monitor vendor performance against KPIs (conversion, member satisfaction, redemption rates) and hold partners accountable to agreed standards

 - Manage contract renewals, offboarding underperforming vendors, and continuously refreshing the catalog

Merchandising

 - Curate and manage the Forma store catalog, ensuring a compelling, relevant, and on-brand product and service mix

 - Develop merchandising strategies that drive engagement, discovery, and spend across benefit categories

 - Identify gaps in the catalog and proactively source new vendors to fill them

 - Own the presentation of the store aligned to category organization, featured placements, seasonal moments, and promotional campaigns

Cross-Functional Collaboration

 - Partner with Product to ensure a seamless member shopping experience and surface vendor integration needs

 - Collaborate with Finance and Legal on vendor contracts, pricing integrity, and compliance

 - Bring the member voice into vendor decisions — using data, feedback, and trends to inform the catalog strategy




PREFERRED SKILLS

 - 4+ years of experience in merchandising, partnerships, vendor management, or category management — ideally in a marketplace, benefits, HR tech, or consumer platform context

 - Proven track record of sourcing and managing vendor or brand partnerships end-to-end

 - Strong commercial instincts — you know how to negotiate, structure a deal, and assess whether a partner is truly worth the shelf space.

 - Data-driven mindset with the ability to

source_jobs.title: Senior Software Engineer
source_jobs.description:
ABOUT FORMA

The market for employee benefits is broken. Companies spend millions annually on employee benefits that employees neither value nor regularly use. Founded in 2017, Forma set out to build a better model by challenging traditional one-size-fits-all approaches.

Forma’s flexible benefits software helps companies offer competitive benefits packages while reducing costs and inefficiencies, by giving employees more choice and flexibility in how they spend their benefit allowances. The platform also saves HR professionals countless hours managing and supporting various point solutions.

Using Forma, companies can select from a suite of products that include Lifestyle Spending Accounts, Health Spending Accounts, Health Reimbursement Arrangements, Flexible Spending Accounts, and more to design and deliver customized benefits programs–all through a single platform. Employees then have three choices to spend account funds: The Forma Store with discounted products and services, The Forma Visa Card, or claim reimbursement backed by Forma’s world-class member support team.

Forma has helped hundreds of the world’s most admired companies, including Stripe, Zoom, Lululemon, and Affirm, design and support flexible, inclusive benefits programs for nearly a million employees. And, we are seeing great success with 75 NPS, and 98 CSAT ratings from members.

Forma is backed by Emergence Capital and Ribbit Capital and has received numerous awards for its exponential growth, its software innovation, and as a “Great Place to Work.”

 


ABOUT THE ROLE

Build and ship the best possible products accurately and on-time. Solve complex business problems and workflows with highly secure, scalable, and easy-to-use backend APIs and infrastructure. We run our services on GCP and Kubernetes. We integrate with Stripe, Plaid, and others to make complicated financial transactions a breeze.

 


YOU WILL

 - Release new product features and maintain existing services

 - Have a deep understanding about product values and contribute collectively to create the best product experience

 - Build secure, performant, and easy-to-use backend APIs and database designs with clear documentation

 - Help improve engineering processes and standards to improve efficiency and product quality

 


PREFERRED SKILLS

 - 5+ years working with Kotlin / NodeJS / Typescript and building APIs

 - Familiar with NoSql and / or relational databases

 - Familiar with TypeScript / JavaScript or similar languages

 - Familiar with agile methodologies, and have led a project from end-to-end

 - Experience at an early-stage startup is a plus

 - Fintech experience a plus

 


BENEFITS AND PERKS

 - Remote-first working environment

 - Medical, dental and vision insurance plans

 - Employee wellness program

 - One-time home office stipend

 - 401(k) savings plan

 - Flexible PTO policy

 - 12 weeks Parental Leave + 4 additional weeks for the Birthing Parent

 

At Forma, we value diversity, and always treat all employees and job applicants based on merit, qualifications, competence, and talent. We do not discriminate on the basis of race, religion, color, national origin, gender, sexual orientation, age, marital status, veteran status, or disability status.

Duties and responsibilities may not all be covered in the description, or may change over time at the discretion of Forma. You're encouraged to apply even if your experience doesn't precisely match the job description. Your skills and passion will stand out—and set you apart—especially if your career has taken some extraordinary twists and turns. At Forma, we welcome diverse perspectives, and people who think rigorously / aren't afraid to challenge assumptions. Join us!

source_jobs.title: Customer Operations Specialist
source_jobs.description:
ABOUT FORMA

The market for employee benefits is broken. Companies spend millions annually on employee benefits that employees neither value nor regularly use. Founded in 2017, Forma set out to build a better model by challenging traditional one-size-fits-all approaches.

Forma’s flexible benefits software helps companies offer competitive benefits packages while reducing costs and inefficiencies, by giving employees more choice and flexibility in how they spend their benefit allowances. The platform also saves HR professionals countless hours managing and supporting various point solutions.

Using Forma, companies can select from a suite of products that include Lifestyle Spending Accounts, Health Spending Accounts, Health Reimbursement Arrangements, Flexible Spending Accounts, and more to design and deliver customized benefits programs–all through a single platform. Employees then have three choices to spend account funds: The Forma Store with discounted products and services, The Forma Visa Card, or claim reimbursement backed by Forma’s world-class member support team.

Forma has helped hundreds of the world’s most admired companies, including Stripe, Zoom, Lululemon, and Affirm, design and support flexible, inclusive benefits programs for nearly a million employees. And, we are seeing great success with 98% customer retention, 75 NPS, and 98 CSAT ratings from members.

Forma is backed by Emergence Capital and Ribbit Capital and has received numerous awards for its exponential growth, its software innovation, and as a “Great Place to Work.”

 

We are looking for a Customer Operations Specialist for our Platform Operations team. You will manage the operational lifecycle of our post-tax and pre-tax accounts and serve as the operational "control center" for our customer support team. Beyond daily troubleshooting and task execution, you will focus on building and optimizing workflows to empower our cross-functional partners. We are looking for a proactive problem-solver who can anticipate product or service issues before they arise and who brings a "can-do" attitude to delivering world-class customer service.




YOU WILL

 - Serve as a Forma product expert to quickly resolve customer concerns and provide tailored recommendations, including identifying underlying issues in account setup or the product.

 - Own daily operational tasks, including account configuration, program policy updates, and customer onboarding or expansion support.

 - Continuous improvement: Identify, implement, and optimize processes for maximum efficiency and quality.

 - Translate customer feedback into actionable product roadmap recommendations through collaboration with product and engineering teams.

 - Track, analyze, and report on operational performance metrics.




PREFERRED SKILLS

 - Bachelor's degree or equivalent work experience preferred.

 - Excellent written and verbal English communication skills.

 - Experience in a fast-paced tech startup and fully remote work environment is preferred.

 - Experience in employee benefits is a strong plus.

 - Proven ability to build new processes and deliver end-to-end projects with minimal supervision.

 - Strong independent and data-driven thinking, with the ability to break down ambiguous goals and analyze operational metrics.

 - Flexibility to coordinate with global teams, and occasional peak-period monitoring.




BENEFITS AND PERKS

 - Remote-first working environment

 - MacBook

 - $100 USD/mo - BeWell funds

 - $300 USD - One time subsidy to build your home office

 - $2000 USD/year - BeHealthy funds



At Forma, we value diversity, and always treat all employees and job applicants based on merit, qualifications, competence, and talent. We do not discriminate on the basis of race, religion, color, national origin, gender, sexual orientation, age, marital status, veteran status, or disability status.

source_jobs.title: Senior Software Engineer I, Product Experience
source_jobs.description:
ABOUT FORMA

The market for employee benefits is broken. Companies spend millions annually on employee benefits that employees neither value nor regularly use. Founded in 2017, Forma set out to build a better model by challenging traditional one-size-fits-all approaches.

Forma’s flexible benefits software helps companies offer competitive benefits packages while reducing costs and inefficiencies, by giving employees more choice and flexibility in how they spend their benefit allowances. The platform also saves HR professionals countless hours managing and supporting various point solutions.

Using Forma, companies can select from a suite of products that include Lifestyle Spending Accounts, Health Spending Accounts, Health Reimbursement Arrangements, Flexible Spending Accounts, and more to design and deliver customized benefits programs–all through a single platform. Employees then have three choices to spend account funds: The Forma Store with discounted products and services, The Forma Visa Card, or claim reimbursement backed by Forma’s world-class member support team.

Forma has helped hundreds of the world’s most admired companies, including Stripe, Zoom, Lululemon, and Affirm, design and support flexible, inclusive benefits programs for nearly a million employees. And, we are seeing great success with 98% customer retention, 75 NPS, and 98 CSAT ratings from members.

Forma is backed by Emergence Capital and Ribbit Capital and has received numerous awards for its exponential growth, its software innovation, and as a “Great Place to Work.”




ABOUT THE ROLE

Join our Product Experience (PX) team and help build the systems behind Forma's member and admin experience - the member portal, admin portal, mobile app, and the integrations that power them. You'll work across the stack to deliver intuitive, secure, and scalable experiences that members and customer admins rely on every day.

This role is fullstack-preferred, but we're also open to strong backend engineers who are eager to grow into fullstack work.




YOU WILL

 - Design, build, and maintain backend services that power Forma's benefits platform — including APIs for account lifecycle, transactions (claims, reimbursements, contributions), and member management.

 - Work across React/TypeScript/NestJS services, contributing to whichever codebase the work requires.

 - Build and consume event-driven integrations using Google Pub/Sub, enabling reliable communication between services.

 - Write well-tested, production-ready code and take ownership of your services in production - including on-call responsibilities, monitoring (Datadog), and incident response.

 - Collaborate closely with product, ops, customer success teams and engineers across the different teams to deliver features that serve members, customer admins, and internal operations teams.

 - Contribute to engineering standards, tooling, and processes that help the team ship faster and more reliably.

 - Use AI tools thoughtfully as part of your workflow - we encourage engineers to leverage AI assistants to accelerate development, improve code quality, and explore solutions faster, while maintaining strong judgment and ownership of the work they ship.




PREFERRED SKILLS

 - 2–5 years of professional experience building backend services and APIs.

 - Strong proficiency in TypeScript/NestJS

 - Solid experience with relational databases, particularly PostgreSQL - schema design, query optimization, and migrations.

 - Experience with event-driven architectures or messaging systems (e.g., Pub/Sub, Kafka, SQS/SNS) is a strong plus.

 - Familiarity with cloud infrastructure - GCP preferred, but AWS or Azure experience translates well. Bonus points for experience with Kubernetes, Terraform, or CI/CD pipelines.

 - Frontend experience with React and TypeScript (strongly preferred). Familiarity with MUI, Tailwind, or component-driven UI development is a plus.

 - Comfortable with observability tools (logging, metric

notes: Small job board with a mix of tech and non-tech roles, indicating a product-heavy company with a strong engineering core.
